body.title: [awaiting_validation] Reconstruct/freeze 52 palace-pointer cert-walks tickets from echoes TSV
body.description:
**Problem:** `~/TheIgors-cert-walks/W1/lab/theigors/theigors/tickets/` has 52 numeric/GH-prefixed ticket files that are hollow pointers ('Canonical row: clan.memories id=N') — their bodies died with the retired palace DB. `~/TheIgorsProject/20260427.Cleanup/database/palace_old_ticket_echoes.tsv` (161 rows) carries title+size for the numeric ids; `old_done_tickets.tsv` carries T-slug+status+title+date. Folds in T-cleanup-investigate-cert-walks.
**Proposed shape:** Cross-join the two TSVs to recover what survives for each of the 52 (id, title, size, status, date) and write metadata-stub ticket nodes into UU tickets/ CLEARLY marked `palace-era, body-lost` so they are honest stubs, not fake-complete tickets. Any of the 52 with NO recoverable metadata in either TSV: leave in the cert-walks snapshot for the freeze, logged as unrecoverable. Honesty over completeness (CP1): a stub that admits the body is gone beats a fabricated body.
**Affected files:** writes to devlab/runtime/memory/tickets/ (metadata stubs); reads cert-walks tickets + the two TSV dumps. No code changes.
**Design rules:** NO SQLITE (TSV/.md -> flat JSON; dead palace DB not queried); one-home store; CP1 honesty (stubs marked body-lost, never fabricated); [[reference_cc_queue_list_hides_tickets]] — verify via store not just list.
**Scope boundary:** IN — the 52 numeric/GH cert-walks pointer tickets + TSV metadata recovery. OUT — the 645 cert-walks T- tickets (100% already in UU); full-body reconstruction (bodies are unrecoverable — do not invent them).
**Test plan:** (1) Each of the 52 ids resolves to either a UU metadata-stub node (marked palace-era/body-lost) or a logged 'unrecoverable, frozen in snapshot' entry. (2) Spot-check 5 stubs: title/size/date match the TSV row; body explicitly states it is a palace-era metadata stub. (3) No stub presents a fabricated body.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Investigated: all 52 cert-walks numeric/GH pointer tickets (26 numeric + 26 GH-) are ALREADY in the UU store with REAL content (e.g. 122->'Containerization eval', GH-151->'Epic: Productization'), absorbed in an earlier-generation migration (cc.0.<id>.20260324 files). Precise exact-id dedup: 0 of 52 missing. The cert-walks pointer .md files are hollow duplicates -> nothing to reconstruct; they freeze with the W1 snapshot in T-freeze-dead-code-to-theigorsproject. Reverses the discovery agent's '52 unique' estimate (it lacked the precise UU id set). No files changed (verification only).
body.no_commit_reason: verification-only: all 52 already absorbed; no reconstruction needed
